mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL above 240 implies inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE (<4%) indicates tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profits and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when UNRATE remains below 4% for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output that supports corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher HOUST values indicate increased housing demand that stimulates economic activity and equity market performance, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns despite HOUST rising above 1200 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth exceeding 150k in a month signals a strong labor market that boosts consumer spending and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when PAYEMS exceeds 150k for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL combined with low FEDFUNDS indicates stagflation risks that depress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns despite CPIAUCSL > 240 and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.
